sections.paragraph:
fem. רְחָבָה adj.-

(1) broad, wide, Job 30:14 of the sea, Job 11:9 (opp. to long); used of a wall (where it refers to thickness), Jer 51:58 Neh 3:8 also long and broad, spacious, of the earth, Exo 3:8 Neh 9:35 of a cup large around (opp. to deep, Eze 23:32); which latter is expressed by its own proper formula; רְחַב יָדַיִם, רַחֲבַת יָדיִם “large on every side,” i.e. extending widely every way, long and broad; spoken of land, Gen 34:21 Isa 33:21 of sea, Psa 104:25. The signification is sometimes still wider, and comprehends also height or depth; like the Lat. amplus; at least in metaphorical expressions, as, Psa 119:96, “thy commandment is exceeding broad,” i.e. the law is copious and infinite; also רְחַב לֵב Psa 101:5 רְחַב נֶפֶשׁ Pro 28:25, inflated, i.e. proud; and with a preceding subst., pride, Pro 21:4.

(2) רָחָב [Rahab], pr.n. of a harlot at Jericho, Jos 2:1 6:17 .
